title: Amiloride and Hydrochlorothiazide: MedlinePlus Drug Information
text: cist if you are allergic to amiloride, hydrochlorothiazide, sulfonamide containing medications, any other medications, or any of the ingredients in amiloride and hydrochlorothiazide tablets. Ask your pharmacist or check the patient information for a list of the ingredients. Some medications should not be taken with amiloride and hydrochlorothiazide. Other medications may cause dosing changes or extra monitoring when taken with amiloride and hydrochlorothiazide. Make sure you have discussed any medications you are currently taking or plan to take before starting amiloride and hydrochlorothiazide with your doctor and pharmacist. Before starting, stopping, or changing any medications while taking amiloride and hydrochlorothiazide, please get the advice of your doctor or pharmacist.. if you are taking cholestyramine or colestipol, take them 1 hour before or 4 hours after taking amiloride and hydrochlorothiazide. The following nonprescription products may interact with amiloride and hydrochlorothiazide: potassium supplements. potassium containing medication supplements,hydrocortisone, and nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs)[ ibuprofen (Advil, Motrin, others), naproxen (Aleve, Naprosyn, others)]. Be sure to let your doctor and pharmacist know that you are taking these medications before you start taking amiloride and hydrochlorothiazide. Do not start any of these medication
